items.reason: site/index.html and site/docs/index.html render as HTML with navigation, styling, and stylesheet links. OpenAPI pages reference Redoc per evidence (e.g., site/openapi/hosted-json.html with 'redoc'). No href to .md/.yaml/.yml found under site/.

site/index.html contains explicit 'Platform Provides' and 'App Developer Owns' sections with bullets naming OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, Open Badges/CLR, plus app-owned items (pedagogy, UI quality, accessibility, scope minimization).

Each scripts/generate_*.py reads dictionary/*.v1.json and emits SQL comments, OpenAPI JSON, Markdown, and HTML in one run (e.g., generate_oneroster_core.py writes SQL_OUT, OPENAPI_OUT, MARKDOWN_OUT, HTML_OUT from single SOURCE). check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies all surfaces stay synced.

OneRoster, QTI, CASE, Caliper, and integration-governance (covering LTI, Security Framework, Data Privacy) all have substantial dictionary files with many objects/fields (e.g., caliper-core has caliper_envelope, caliper_event, caliper_entity, caliper_actor, etc.). Lead spec accounting documents each.

lead-spec-accounting.md lists categories of covered vs. deferred areas with reasons (e.g., 'Demographics execution' deferred because 'Sensitive data should wait for tenant policy'), but reasons are at category level, not exhaustive per-field. The OneRoster section explicitly admits 'Demographics, Agents/relationships, Resources... not yet executable' and the backlog confirms 'Full OneRoster 1.2' is partial. No explicit per-field sourceStandard references in dictionaries — only dictionary-level source_standard tags.

Sampled fields show plain-language descriptions: 'The platform's stable ID for this person' (people.id), 'The name shown in user interfaces' (people.display_name), 'The first date in the session' (academic_sessions.start_date), 'The OneRoster source ID for this calendar record' (sourced_id). check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces coverage.

demo/server.js implements OneRoster-shaped endpoints; demo/test.js asserts /users/USER-ADA returns sourcedId='USER-ADA' (matching OneRoster 1.2 REST shape) and tests SQL queries, views, and 404 behavior. Tests pass per VERIFY.md.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py issues HTTP requests against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/{people,class_roster,gradebook_results} with the publishable key and validates 200 responses. supabase-hosted-database.md confirms it returned ok:true via PostgREST against the live Postgres backend.

site/index.html embeds a browser SQL console using sql.js loaded from CDN that runs against demo/schema.sql + demo/seed.sql, plus a live API panel that fetches real JSON from site/api/*.json. Portal also includes copy-pasteable curl commands against the live Supabase REST and Edge Function URLs.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md has 10 numbered decisions covering all required areas (Person identity, Class/course/section, Roles, Enrollment vs. membership, Results/scores, Standards alignment, Identifiers, Time/session, Content/resource, Tenancy). Each has named specs, conflict prose, choice, projection table back to each spec, and explicit tradeoff.

Every dictionary field carries a decision_id (e.g., 'DEC-007-identifier-crosswalk'), and standards-overlap-decisions.md contains a machine-readable JSON block under <!-- decision-trace:start --> with produces_fields arrays. check_dictionary_artifacts.py validates bidirectional consistency between decision_ids and produces_fields.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql adds tenant_id to every table, runs ENABLE + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, and creates policies like 'demo_read_people ... USING (tenant_id = COALESCE(NULLIF((auth.jwt() ->> tenant_id), )::uuid, ...))' — predicate references JWT claim, not true. supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json confirms identical predicates. tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py creates two Auth users with different tenant_id app_metadata claims, hits live REST URL, and asserts each can only read its tenant's rows.

dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json contains oauth_scope_policies mapping fine-grained scopes (e.g., 'platform.roster.users.directory:read' with privacy_ceiling 'directory') to specific dictionary field references. Generated OpenAPI emits platformOAuth security scheme, x-oauthScopePolicies, and per-field x-oauthScopes (visible in openapi/generated/integration-governance-core.v0.json operations). Privacy classes are on each field.

Every field sampled in the dictionaries carries a non-empty privacy_class (e.g., 'operational', 'directory', 'education_record', 'behavioral', 'system', 'sensitive', 'public'). check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces required field keys including privacy_class. data-dictionary.seed.json (referenced by site/app.js as privacy_classes) enumerates the vocabulary.

docs/admin-operations.md enumerates two callers — tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py (tenant_rls_test_auth_fixture_setup) and tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py (audit_log_test_auth_fixture_setup) — each with caller, operation, why_user_jwt_insufficient (concrete reasoning about Auth user creation requirements), and last_reviewed: 2026-04-26 (within 30 days of the 2026-04-24 research date). Inline comments in test files reference the operation IDs.

Migration creates public.audit_log with tenant_id, client_id, scope, purpose, field_accessed, subject_table, subject_id, timestamp columns plus the read_people_sensitive_audited(...) SECURITY DEFINER function that inserts one audit row per restricted field before returning data. supabase/functions/audited-roster-read/index.ts wraps this. tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py hits the live Edge Function URL and verifies audit rows for people.display_name/given_name/family_name/email contain all required claims.

docs/spec-gap-backlog.md contains an explicit 'Rubric Trace For Open Items' table mapping rubric IDs (lead_spec_full_accounting, tenant_isolation_enforced, oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields, audit_log_for_sensitive_reads, edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints, edge_functions_propagate_user_jwt, rls_enabled_on_referenced_tables) to backlog item numbers, status, target slice, and owner notes.

docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md has explicit columns for Structured source, Generated artifacts, Runnable slice, and Unsupported ledger per spec area. Populated honestly — e.g., QTI/CASE/Caliper rows show 'No' under Runnable slice while OneRoster core shows 'Yes'.

Two non-CRUD Edge Functions exist (gradebook-bulk-submit, audited-roster-read), each with deno.json and index.ts that forward the user JWT, plus curl examples in docs/supabase-hosted-database.md. However, the rubric's named non-CRUD set (gradebook bulk submit, LTI 1.3 launch handler, Caliper event ingestion, OAuth token exchange) is only partially covered — LTI launch, Caliper ingestion, and OAuth token exchange are not implemented (backlog confirms items 20-22 remain).

Both Edge Functions construct the Supabase client from the request Authorization header (`global: { headers: { Authorization: authorization } }` in gradebook-bulk-submit; same in audited-roster-read) and use SUPABASE_ANON_KEY/SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, never SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. docs/admin-operations.md exists and lists no Edge Function as a service-role caller.

supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json shows every OneRoster fact table (organizations, people, academic_sessions, courses, classes, enrollments, line_items, results, source_identifiers, audit_log) with rowsecurity=true, forceRowSecurity=true, and policies whose `using` clauses reference auth.jwt() tenant_id claim — never literal `true`. generatedAt: 2026-04-26T11:07:15Z. CASE/QTI/Caliper/LTI tables are not yet runtime-backed, so this scope matches what's deployed.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py validates HTTP 200 from /people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results against the live Supabase project URL using the publishable key, hitting PostgREST against Postgres (not static JSON). docs/supabase-hosted-database.md documents verified live status on 2026-04-26.

site/index.html includes a copy-pasteable curl block: `curl 'https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/people?select=id,display_name&order=id.asc&limit=1' -H 'apikey: sb_publishable_...' -H 'authorization: Bearer sb_publishable_...'` using only the public publishable key documented in the portal.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. scripts/evaluate_platform.py writes a report containing per-rubric `items` array (id/status/reason/evidence_paths/blocked_prerequisites) plus a `counts` summary with pass/partial/fail/blocked/total and a `done` flag.

scripts/check_spec_conformance.py still runs in VERIFY.md and writes site/api/spec-conformance.json with `metric: score_gate, notACompletionClaim: true, scoreScope: 'Advisory ... Does NOT gate the Codex loop'`. scripts/codex_loop.py's maybe_publish() gates on judge_result and rubric_progressed (LLM evaluator output), with the advisory score only used as a fallback when evaluator data is unavailable.

scripts/codex_loop.py implements read_overrides() reading docs/loop-overrides.md, overrides_paused() that halts on a bare `PAUSE` line BEFORE the iteration runs, and format_overrides_for_prompt() that injects file contents under a 'Human overrides' label in the prompt. However, docs/loop-overrides.md itself is not present in the evidence (only referenced via site/docs/loop-overrides.html link). Cannot confirm the required `## Pause`, `## Decisions`, `## Unblocks` sections exist.

scripts/codex_loop.py main() returns 0 when `after_eval.get('done')` is true, both before iteration starts and after each iteration. Termination references the LLM evaluator's `done` flag (set by evaluate_platform.py when no fail/partial items remain and any blocked items have prerequisites). Hard caps preserved: --max-iterations (DEFAULT 25), --stall-limit (DEFAULT 3 consecutive iterations without rubric progress), --codex-timeout-minutes.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
